lines: Good morning, brave legs. Keeper Brindle keeps the village road-cake ledger, and I have spilled ink on the important page.
Ask him for the road word, then bring it back before the bell forgets what hour it is.
Good morning, brave legs. I smell Oswin's oats on your pack, which means the stable has already blessed your feet.
Keeper Brindle still keeps the road-cake ledger. Ask him for the road word, then bring it back before the bell forgets what hour it is.
Keeper Brindle will know which cake is meant for wandering. I shall stand here looking useful until you return.
Bread in pocket, road word remembered, courage accounted for. That is a proper morning.
Tell Oldwood we are not afraid of its shadows, only politely suspicious.
The ledger is dry, the cake is counted, and the road has your name written neatly in the margin.
I keep the village ledger because the village keeps wandering off into adventures.
